title: Automated connected vehicle control system architecture
abstract: A variety of controllers, control architectures, systems, methods and algorithms are described for controlling a host vehicle's participation in a platoon. In various embodiments, a vehicle platooning control system includes at least two of (i) a platoon controller, (ii) a gateway processor, and (iii) a vehicle interface controller. The platoon controller is configured to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle to platoon with a platoon partner. The gateway processor coordinates communications between a host vehicle and the platoon partner. The vehicle interface controller manages communications between the platoon controller and one or more host vehicle control units. The vehicle interface controller may also include a safety monitor that includes one or more safety monitoring algorithms that verify that platooning is safe.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A connected vehicle control system for at least partially automatically controlling a host vehicle based at least in part on information received from a second vehicle, the connected vehicle control system comprising: a connected vehicle controller configured to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle; a gateway processor arranged to coordinate communications between a host vehicle and the second vehicle, the gateway processor further being configured to communicate with the connected vehicle controller; and a vehicle interface controller arranged to manage communications between the connected vehicle controller and one or more host vehicle control units, the vehicle interface controller being configured to receive the torque and braking requests from the connected vehicle controller and to communicate corresponding torque and braking requests to the appropriate host vehicle control unit(s), the vehicle interface controller including a safety monitor that includes one or more safety monitoring algorithms that, during at least partially automated driving, verify that commands received from the connected vehicle controller are safe. 2. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 1 wherein the vehicle interface controller is at least ASIL-C compliant. 3. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 2 wherein the connected vehicle controller and the gateway processor have an ASIL rating that is lower than an ASIL rating of the vehicle interface controller. 4. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 3 wherein the connected vehicle controller and the gateway processor are QM rated under ISO 26262. 5. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 1 wherein an interface between the connected vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the connected vehicle controller' s torque request; the connected vehicle controller' s brake request; and 

 verified second vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the second vehicle, wherein the safety monitor utilizes the second vehicle state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm. 6. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 5 wherein the interface between the connected vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the connected vehicle controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the second vehicle via the gateway processor. 7. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 5 wherein the verified second vehicle state information is information sent and verified by a vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle, and wherein the verified second vehicle state information is: received by the gateway processor from the second vehicle; passed from the gateway processor to the connected vehicle controller without modification by the gateway processor; passed from the connected vehicle controller to the vehicle interface controller without modification by the connected vehicle controller; used by the connected vehicle controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms. 8. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 7 wherein the gateway processor is further configured to receive unverified state information from the second vehicle, the unverified second vehicle state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the second vehicle, wherein the unverified second vehicle state information is passed to the connected vehicle controller and used by the connected vehicle controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests and wherein the unverified second vehicle state information is not passed to the vehicle interface controller. 9. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 5 wherein: the verified second vehicle state information is passed in second vehicle state information data blocks, each second vehicle state information data block including a checksum applied by the vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle; and the host vehicle vehicle interface controller is configured to utilize the checksums to verify the integrity of the second vehicle state information. 10. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 1 for use on a vehicle having one or more vehicle communication buses, wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured as a listener capable of receiving messages transmitted on at least one of the vehicle communication buses, but is not capable of transmitting messages onto any of the vehicle communication buses; the vehicle interface controller is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the vehicle communication buses; and the gateway processor is not coupled to any of the vehicle buses and is not capable of receiving or transmitting any messages on any of the vehicle buses. 11. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 10 wherein the one or more vehicle buses are Controller Area Network (CAN) buses. 12. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 1 wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured as a first system on module (SOM); the gateway processor is configured as second system on module (SOM); and the vehicle interface controller is implemented as a single packaged integrated circuit. 13. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 1 wherein the connected vehicle controller is a platoon controller and the second vehicle is a platoon partner and the safety monitoring algorithms are configured to verify that platooning is safe during platooning. 14. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 13 wherein an interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the platoon controller' s torque request; the platoon controller' s brake request; and 

 verified partner state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the partner vehicle, wherein the safety monitor utilizes the partner state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm. 15. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 14 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a platoon state indicator that indicates when the platoon controller believes its torque and braking requests should be directing operation of the host vehicle. 16. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 14 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a platoon controller retard request that designates a desired level of retarder based braking. 17. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 14 further comprising a driver interface device arranged to receive driver commands to authorize platooning and to disengage a platoon, the driver interface device being configured to communicate platoon authorization and platoon disengage commands to the vehicle interface controller. 18. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 17 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a driver interface device state indicator indicative of a commands inputted by a driver using the driver interface device. 19. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 14 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a platoon controller steering request that conveys steering commands from the platoon controller to the vehicle interface controller. 20. A vehicle control system for at least partially automatically controlling a host vehicle, the vehicle control system comprising: a vehicle controller configured to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle; and a vehicle interface controller arranged to manage communications between the vehicle controller and one or more host vehicle control units, the vehicle interface controller being configured to receive the torque and braking requests from the vehicle controller and to communicate corresponding torque and braking requests to 

 the appropriate host vehicle control unit(s), the vehicle interface controller including a safety monitor that, during at least partially automated driving, verify that commands received from the vehicle controller are safe. 21. A vehicle control system as recited in claim 20 wherein the vehicle interface controller is ASIL compliant and the vehicle controller is QM rated or has an ASIL rating that is lower than an ASIL rating of the vehicle interface controller. 22. A vehicle control system as recited in claim 20 for use on a vehicle having one or more vehicle communication buses, wherein: the vehicle controller is configured as a listener capable of receiving messages transmitted on at least one of the vehicle communication buses, but is not capable of transmitting messages onto any of the vehicle communication buses; and the vehicle interface controller is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the vehicle communication buses. 23. A vehicle control system as recited in claim 20 wherein an interface between the vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the vehicle controller's torque request; the vehicle controller' s brake request; and verified second vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of a second vehicle, wherein the safety monitor receives the verified second vehicle state information from the vehicle controller and utilizes the second vehicle state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm; and verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the vehicle controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the partner vehicle. 24. A vehicle control system as recited in claim 23 wherein the verified second vehicle state information is information sent and verified by a vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle, and wherein the verified second vehicle state information is: received a by the vehicle controller in verified second vehicle state information data blocks, each verified second vehicle state information data block 

 including a checksum applied by a vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms, the vehicle interface controller further utilizing the checksums to verify the integrity of the partner state information. 25. A vehicle platooning control system for controlling a host vehicle's participation in a platoon that includes the host vehicle and a platoon partner vehicle, the vehicle platooning control system comprising: a platoon controller configured to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle to platoon with the platoon partner vehicle; and a vehicle interface controller arranged to manage communications between the platoon controller and one or more host vehicle control units, the vehicle interface controller being configured to receive the torque and braking requests from the platoon controller and to communicate corresponding torque and braking requests to the appropriate host vehicle control unit(s), the vehicle interface controller including a safety monitor that includes one or more safety monitoring algorithms that, during platooning, verify that platooning is safe. 26. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 25 wherein the vehicle interface controller is at least ASIL-C compliant and the platoon controller is QM rated under ISO 26262. 27. A vehicle platoon control system as recited in claim 25 for use on a vehicle having one or more vehicle communication buses, wherein: the platoon controller is configured as a listener capable of receiving messages transmitted on at least one of the vehicle communication buses, but is not capable of transmitting messages onto any of the vehicle communication buses; and the vehicle interface controller is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the vehicle communication buses. 28. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 25 wherein an interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the platoon controller' s torque request; the platoon controller' s brake request; 

 verified partner state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the platoon partner, wherein the safety monitor receives the verified partner state information from the platoon controller and utilizes the partner state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm; and verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the platoon controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the partner vehicle. 29. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 28 wherein the verified partner state information is information sent and verified by a vehicle interface controller on the partner vehicle, and wherein the verified partner state information is: received a by the platoon controller in verified partner state information data blocks, each verified partner state information data block including a checksum applied by the vehicle interface controller on the partner vehicle; passed without modification from the platoon controller to the vehicle interface controller; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms, the vehicle interface controller further utilizing the checksums to verify the integrity of the partner state information. 30. A vehicle platooning and control system as recited in claim 29 wherein the platoon controller is further configured to receive unverified partner state information from the partner vehicle, the unverified partner state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the partner vehicle, wherein the unverified partner state information is used by the platoon controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests and wherein the unverified partner state information is not passed to the vehicle interface controller. 31. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 28 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a platoon state indicator that indicates when the platoon controller believes its torque and braking requests should be directing operation of the host vehicle. 32. A vehicle platoon control system as recited in claim 27 wherein the one or more vehicle buses are Controller Area Network (CAN) buses. 33. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 25 wherein: the platoon controller is configured as a system on module (SOM); and the vehicle interface controller is implemented as a single packaged integrated circuit. 34. A method of transmitting ASIL verified sensor data between vehicles participating in a connected driving session including two or more vehicles, the method comprising: creating a data block that includes ASIL compliant sensor data using an ASIL certified component on a first vehicle, the data block including a data verification code; transmitting the data block of ASIL compliant sensor data to a non-ASIL certified component on the first vehicle; transmitting the data block of ASIL compliant sensor data from the non-ASIL certified component on the first vehicle to a non-ASIL certified component of the second vehicle; transmitting the data block of ASIL compliant sensor data from the non-ASIL certified component of the second vehicle to an ASIL certified component on the second vehicle; and wherein the ASIL certified component on the second vehicle verifies the integrity of the ASIL compliant sensor data using the data verification code and uses at least some of the verified ASIL compliant sensor data in at least one safety monitoring algorithm related to the platoon. 35. A method as recited in claim 34 wherein the data verification code is a checksum. 36. A method as recited in claim 34 wherein the ASIL certified components on the first and second vehicles vehicle interface controllers on the first and second vehicle respectively and the non-ASIL certified components are platoon controllers on the first and second vehicles respectively. 37. A method as recited in claim 36 wherein the data block of ASIL compliant sensor data is transmitted from the platoon controller on the first vehicle to a gateway 

 on the first vehicle, and from the gateway on the first vehicle to a gateway on the second vehicle and from the gateway on the second vehicle to the platoon controller on the second vehicle, all without modification. 38. A vehicle platooning control system for controlling a host vehicle's participation in a platoon that includes the host vehicle and a platoon partner vehicle, the host vehicle having one or more host vehicle communication buses, the vehicle platooning control system comprising: a platoon controller configured to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle to platoon with the platoon partner vehicle, wherein the platoon controller is configured to receive sensed information from one or more host sensors on the host vehicle, at least some of the sensed information being received over one of the host vehicle communication buses; a gateway processor arranged to coordinate communications between a host vehicle and the platoon partner, the gateway processor being configured to (i) receive partner state information from the platoon partner and pass the partner state information to the platoon partner for use in determining the torque and braking requests, the partner state information being indicative of at least a speed, a braking level, and a GNSS position of the platoon partner; and (ii) receive host vehicle state information from the platoon controller and pass the host vehicle state information to the platoon partner, the host vehicle state information being indicative of at least a speed, a braking level and a GNSS position of the host vehicle, and wherein the gateway processor is not coupled to any of the host vehicle communication buses and is not capable of transmitting any messages on any of the vehicle communication buses or directly receiving sensor information from any of the host sensors; and a dedicated wired communications link that directly connects the platoon controller and the gateway processor, wherein no other devices are coupled to the communications link. 39. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 wherein: the gateway processor is configured to wirelessly communicate with the platoon partner using a short-range, vehicle-to-vehicle wireless communications protocol; 

 the gateway processor is further configured to communicate with a networks operations center using a second wireless communications protocol selected from the group consisting of a cellular communications protocol and a satellite communications protocol; and the platoon controller and the gateway processor communicate over the dedicated communication link using an Ethernet protocol. 40. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 wherein: the platoon controller is configured as a first system on module (SOM); and the gateway processor is configured as second system on module (SOM). 41. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 further comprising a display mounted in a cabin of the host vehicle, wherein: the gateway processor is further configured to receive a video stream from the partner vehicle and forward the video stream to the display, the video stream showing a view in front of the partner vehicle; and the video stream is not passed to the platoon controller. 42. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 wherein: the gateway processor further comprising a message logger, the message logger being configured to log partner state information and host state information passed through the gateway processor; and the platoon controller does not have any logging capabilities; and wherein the platoon controller further passes the torque and braking requests to the gateway controller and the message logger logs all of the torque and braking requests generated by the platoon controller. 43. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 further comprising a vehicle interface controller arranged to manage communications between the platoon controller and one or more host vehicle control units, the vehicle interface controller being configured to receive the torque and braking requests from the platoon controller and to communicate corresponding torque and braking requests to the appropriate host vehicle control unit(s), the vehicle interface controller including a safety monitor that includes one or more safety monitoring algorithms that, during platooning, verify that platooning is safe, and wherein the vehicle interface controller 

 is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the host vehicle communication buses. 44. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 43 wherein: the vehicle interface controller is at least ASIL-C compliant; and the platoon controller and the gateway processor are QM rated under ISO 26262. 45. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 44 wherein: the gateway processor receives verified partner state information from the partner vehicle that is verified by a vehicle interface controller on the partner vehicle and passes the verified partner state information to the platoon controller without modification by the gateway processor; the vehicle interface controller uses the verified partner state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms; the verified partner state information is passed in partner state information data blocks, each partner state information data block including a checksum applied by the vehicle interface controller on the partner vehicle; and the host vehicle vehicle interface controller is configured to utilize the checksum to verify the integrity of the partner state information. 46. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 45 wherein the gateway processor is further configured to receive unverified partner state information from the partner vehicle, the unverified partner state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the partner vehicle, wherein the unverified partner state information is passed to the platoon controller and used by the platoon controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests ad wherein the unverified partner state information is not passed to the vehicle interface controller. 47. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 43 wherein the one or more host vehicle buses are Controller Area Network (CAN) buses. 48. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 43 wherein the gateway processor further comprises a message logger, the message logger being configured to log state information passed through the gateway processor, the logged state information including: 

 verified partner state information received from the partner vehicle that is verified by a vehicle interface controller on the partner vehicle; unverified partner state information received from the partner vehicle, the unverified partner state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the partner vehicle; verified host vehicle state information that is verified by the vehicle interface controller on the host vehicle; and unverified host vehicle state information received from the platoon controller, the unverified host vehicle state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the host vehicle. 49. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 48 wherein the platoon controller does not have any message logging capability and is arranged to pass the torque and braking requests to the gateway processor and the message logger logs all of the torque and braking requests generated by the platoon controller. 50. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in claim 38 wherein: the gateway processor is configured to receive an authorization to platoon from the network operations center and to indicate such authorization to the platoon controller; and the platoon controller is configured such that it will not initiate platooning unless it has received indication of the authorization to platoon. 51. A connected vehicle control system for at least partially automatically controlling a host vehicle based at least in part on second vehicle current operating state information received from a second vehicle, the connected vehicle control system comprising: a gateway processor arranged to coordinate communications between a host vehicle and the second vehicle and to receive the second vehicle current operating state information from the second vehicle; and a connected vehicle controller configured communicate with the gateway processor and to determine torque and braking requests for at least partially automatically controlling the host vehicle based at least in part on the second vehicle current operating state information. 52. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 wherein the gateway processor receives the second vehicle current operating state information from the second vehicle and passes the second vehicle current operating state information to the connected vehicle controller for use in determining the torque and braking requests, the second vehicle current operating state information being indicative of at least a speed, a braking level and a position of the second vehicle. 53. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 52 wherein the gateway processor receives host vehicle state information from the connected vehicle controller and passes the host vehicle state information to the second vehicle, the host vehicle state information being indicative of at least a speed, a braking level and a position of the host vehicle. 54. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 for use on a host vehicle having one or more host vehicle communication buses, wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured to receive sensed information from one or more host sensors on the host vehicle, wherein at least some of the sensed information is received over one of the host vehicle communication buses; and the gateway processor is not coupled to any of the host vehicle communication buses and is not capable of transmitting any messages on any of the vehicle communication buses or directly receiving host vehicle sensor information from any of the host sensors. 55. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 wherein: the gateway processor is configured to wirelessly communicate with the second vehicle using a short-range, vehicle-to-vehicle wireless communications protocol; and the gateway processor is further configured to communicate with a networks operations center using a second wireless communications protocol selected from the group consisting of a cellular communications protocol and a satellite communications protocol. 56. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured as a first system on module (SOM); and the gateway processor is configured as second system on module (SOM). 57. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 further comprising a dedicated wired communications link that directly connects the connected vehicle controller and the gateway processor, wherein no other devices are coupled to the dedicated communications link. 58. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 57 wherein the wired communication link is selected from the group consisting of a co-axial cable; a twisted pair wiring and a fiber optic link. 59. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 57 wherein the connected vehicle controller and the gateway processor communicate over the dedicated communication link using an Ethernet protocol. 60. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 51 further comprising a display mounted in a cabin of the host vehicle, wherein: the gateway processor is further configured to receive a video stream from the partner vehicle and forward the video stream to the display; and the video stream is not passed to the connected vehicle controller. 61. A connected vehicle controller as recited in claim 53 wherein: the gateway processor further comprises a message logger, the message logger being configured to log the second vehicle current operating state information and the host state information passed through the gateway processor; and the connected vehicle controller does not have any logging capabilities. 62. A connected vehicle controller as recited in claim 61 wherein the connected vehicle controller further passes the torque and braking requests to the gateway controller and the message logger logs all of the torque and braking requests generated by the connected vehicle controller. 63. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 1-4 wherein an interface between the connected vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the connected vehicle controller' s torque request; the connected vehicle controller' s brake request; and verified second vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the second vehicle, wherein the safety monitor utilizes the second vehicle state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm. 64. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 63 wherein the interface between the connected vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the connected vehicle controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the second vehicle via the gateway processor. 65. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 63 or 64 wherein the verified second vehicle state information is information sent and verified by a vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle, and wherein the verified second vehicle state information is: received by the gateway processor from the second vehicle; passed from the gateway processor to the connected vehicle controller without modification by the gateway processor; passed from the connected vehicle controller to the vehicle interface controller without modification by the connected vehicle controller; used by the connected vehicle controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms. 66. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 63-65 wherein the gateway processor is further configured to receive unverified state information from the second vehicle, the unverified second vehicle state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the second vehicle, wherein the unverified second vehicle state information is passed to the connected vehicle controller and used by the connected vehicle controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests and wherein the unverified second vehicle state information is not passed to the vehicle interface controller. 67. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 63-66 wherein: the verified second vehicle state information is passed in second vehicle state information data blocks, each second vehicle state information data block including a checksum applied by the vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle; and 

 the host vehicle vehicle interface controller is configured to utilize the checksums to verify the integrity of the second vehicle state information. 68. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 1-9, 12-19 or 63-67 for use on a vehicle having one or more vehicle communication buses, wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured as a listener capable of receiving messages transmitted on at least one of the vehicle communication buses, but is not capable of transmitting messages onto any of the vehicle communication buses; the vehicle interface controller is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the vehicle communication buses; and the gateway processor is not coupled to any of the vehicle buses and is not capable of receiving or transmitting any messages on any of the vehicle buses. 69. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 1-11, 13-19 or 63-68 wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured as a first system on module (SOM); the gateway processor is configured as second system on module (SOM); and the vehicle interface controller is implemented as a single packaged integrated circuit. 70. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 1-12 or 63-69 wherein the connected vehicle controller is a platoon controller and the second vehicle is a platoon partner and the safety monitoring algorithms are configured to verify that platooning is safe during platooning. 71. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 70 wherein an interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the platoon controller' s torque request; the platoon controller' s brake request; and verified partner state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the partner vehicle, wherein the safety monitor utilizes the partner state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm. 72. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 71 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further 

 includes a platoon state indicator that indicates when the platoon controller believes its torque and braking requests should be directing operation of the host vehicle. 73. A connected vehicle control system as recited in claim 71 or 72 wherein the interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller further includes a platoon controller retard request that designates a desired level of retarder based braking. 74. A vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 20-21 or 22-24 for use on a vehicle having one or more vehicle communication buses, wherein: the vehicle controller is configured as a listener capable of receiving messages transmitted on at least one of the vehicle communication buses, but is not capable of transmitting messages onto any of the vehicle communication buses; and the vehicle interface controller is configured to transmit and receive messages on at least one of the vehicle communication buses. 75. A vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 20-22 or 74 wherein an interface between the vehicle controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the vehicle controller's torque request; the vehicle controller' s brake request; and verified second vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of a second vehicle, wherein the safety monitor receives the verified second vehicle state information from the vehicle controller and utilizes the second vehicle state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm; and verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the vehicle controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the partner vehicle. 76.  including a checksum applied by a vehicle interface controller on the second vehicle; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms, the vehicle interface controller further utilizing the checksums to verify the integrity of the partner state information. 77. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in any of claims 25-27, 32-33 or 77 wherein an interface between the platoon controller and the vehicle interface controller includes: the platoon controller' s torque request; the platoon controller' s brake request; verified partner state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the platoon partner, wherein the safety monitor receives the verified partner state information from the platoon controller and utilizes the partner state information in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithm; and verified host vehicle state information indicative of at least a speed and a braking level of the host vehicle, wherein the host vehicle state information is transmitted from the vehicle interface controller to the platoon controller for subsequent unmodified transmission to the partner vehicle. 79.  passed without modification from the platoon controller to the vehicle interface controller; and used by the vehicle interface controller in at least one of the safety monitoring algorithms, the vehicle interface controller further utilizing the checksums to verify the integrity of the partner state information. 80. A vehicle platooning and control system as recited in claim 78 or 79 wherein the platoon controller is further configured to receive unverified partner state information from the partner vehicle, the unverified partner state information including at least GNSS position information indicative of a position of the partner vehicle, wherein the unverified partner state information is used by the platoon controller in the determination of the torque and braking requests and wherein the unverified partner state information is not passed to the vehicle interface controller. 81. A vehicle platooning control system as recited in any of claims 38-41, 43-50 or 81-82 wherein: the gateway processor further comprising a message logger, the message logger being configured to log partner state information and host state information passed through the gateway processor; and the platoon controller does not have any logging capabilities; and wherein the platoon controller further passes the torque and braking requests to the gateway controller and the message logger logs all of the torque and braking requests generated by the platoon controller. 84. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 51-53 or 55-62 for use on a host vehicle having one or more host vehicle communication buses, wherein: the connected vehicle controller is configured to receive sensed information from one or more host sensors on the host vehicle, wherein at least some of the sensed information is received over one of the host vehicle communication buses; and the gateway processor is not coupled to any of the host vehicle communication buses and is not capable of transmitting any messages on any of the vehicle communication buses or directly receiving host vehicle sensor information from any of the host sensors. 86. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 51-54, 55-62 or 85 wherein: the gateway processor is configured to wirelessly communicate with the second vehicle using a short-range, vehicle-to-vehicle wireless communications protocol; and the gateway processor is further configured to communicate with a networks operations center using a second wireless communications protocol selected from the group consisting of a cellular communications protocol and a satellite communications protocol. 87. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 51-56, 60-62 or 85-86 further comprising a dedicated wired communications link that directly 

 connects the connected vehicle controller and the gateway processor, wherein no other devices are coupled to the dedicated communications link. 88. A connected vehicle control system as recited in any of claims 51-62 and 85- 87 further comprising a display mounted in a cabin of the host vehicle, wherein: the gateway processor is further configured to receive a video stream from the partner vehicle and forward the video stream to the display; and the video stream is not passed to the connected vehicle controller. 89. A connected vehicle controller as recited in any of claims 53 or 85-88 wherein: the gateway processor further comprises a message logger, the message logger being configured to log the second vehicle current operating state information and the host state information passed through the gateway processor; and the connected vehicle controller does not have any logging capabilities. 90.
